description: Oil & Gas supply-chain layers (upstream through oilfield services). Same schema as supply-chain-layers.json; tickers link to listed equities where applicable.

layers.name: Upstream (E&P)
layers.nickname: The wellhead — reserves, rigs, and resource extraction

companies.name: ExxonMobil
companies.moatSummary: World-class project execution and balance-sheet depth let XOM outspend peers through down-cycles, locking in acreage others must sell.

companies.name: Chevron
companies.moatSummary: Low-cost Permian position and long-term LNG offtake contracts generate resilient FCF well below $50/bbl breakeven.

companies.name: ConocoPhillips
companies.moatSummary: Disciplined capital returns and a sub-$35/bbl cost of supply across a diversified multi-basin portfolio.

companies.name: EOG Resources
companies.moatSummary: Proprietary data and drilling efficiency keep EOG's well costs 15–20% below peers — the closest thing to a technology moat in unconventionals.

companies.name: BP
companies.moatSummary: Legacy deepwater and gas assets fund a multi-decade transition portfolio; execution risk remains the key overhang.

companies.name: Shell
companies.moatSummary: Largest global LNG portfolio and best-in-class commodities trading desk extract margin that pure E&Ps cannot access.

companies.name: Saudi Aramco
companies.moatSummary: Sole legal producer of Saudi reserves — the world's lowest-cost barrel at ~$3/bbl lifting cost with no competitive threat structurally possible.

companies.name: TotalEnergies
companies.moatSummary: Deep African production base and growing LNG position in Qatar and Mozambique underpin a 30-year reserve life.

companies.name: Equinor
companies.moatSummary: Norwegian sovereign backing and best-in-class North Sea operatorship give EQNR a cost of equity that private peers cannot match.

layers.name: Midstream
layers.nickname: The arterial network — pipelines, storage, and LNG terminals

companies.name: Kinder Morgan
companies.moatSummary: 40% of US natural gas flows through KMI infrastructure — fee-based contracts insulate earnings from commodity prices entirely.

companies.name: Enterprise Products
companies.moatSummary: Largest NGL pipeline network in the US with fully integrated fractionation and export terminals — near-impossible to replicate at any cost.

companies.name: Williams Cos.
companies.moatSummary: Transco — the single highest-volume natural gas pipeline in the US — collects regulated tariffs on every molecule moving from Gulf to Northeast.

companies.name: Energy Transfer
companies.moatSummary: Vast multi-commodity network covering crude, NGL, and natural gas across 38 states — scale creates cost advantages no new entrant can close.

companies.name: Enbridge
companies.moatSummary: Mainline system moves ~30% of all North American crude exports; regulated return structure makes it a pipeline utility masquerading as a cyclical.

companies.name: TC Energy
companies.moatSummary: NGTL and Coastal GasLink underpin a regulated asset base with 95%+ contracted cash flows — closest thing to a bond in the energy sector.

layers.name: Downstream
layers.nickname: The conversion layer — crude into fuels, plastics, and chemicals

companies.name: Valero Energy
companies.moatSummary: Scale and complexity advantage across 15 refineries let VLO run the widest range of feedstocks — heavy sour crude discounts drive outsized crack spreads.

companies.name: Phillips 66
companies.moatSummary: Midstream and chemicals segments dampen pure refining cyclicality; CPChem JV is a low-cost petrochemical compounder.

companies.name: Marathon Petroleum
companies.moatSummary: GP economics from MPLX MLP generate recurring fee income that smooths refining margin volatility — effective self-funded midstream toll road.

companies.name: HF Sinclair
companies.moatSummary: Rocky Mountain and mid-continent positioning captures landlocked crude discounts unavailable to coastal peers; Renewables segment adds optionality.

companies.name: Neste
companies.moatSummary: First-mover in hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO) with the lowest carbon-intensity score in the sector — regulatory mandates create captive demand competitors cannot yet serve.

companies.name: Reliance Industries
companies.moatSummary: Jamnagar is the world's largest single-site refinery complex — complexity and scale produce per-barrel margins unachievable at any smaller facility.

layers.name: Oilfield Services & Equipment
layers.nickname: The enabling layer — drilling tech, completion, and subsea systems

companies.name: Schlumberger
companies.moatSummary: Proprietary digital platform (Delfi), deepwater subsea tech, and a service footprint in 120 countries make SLB the operating system that the entire upstream industry runs on.

companies.name: Halliburton
companies.moatSummary: Best-in-class hydraulic fracturing execution in North American unconventionals, but reliant on SLB's technology ecosystem for deepwater and digital capabilities.

companies.name: Baker Hughes
companies.moatSummary: Industrial Internet platform from GE heritage gives BKR a digital edge, but LNG equipment and turbomachinery are the only segments with structural moat independent of SLB.

companies.name: NOV Inc.
companies.moatSummary: Monopoly-like positions in rig components (top drives, iron roughnecks) mean every new rig built globally uses NOV parts — switching cost is a full rig rebuild.

companies.name: TechnipFMC
companies.moatSummary: With Subsea 7, FTI controls the integrated EPCI market for deepwater tiebacks — no deepwater project can be sanctioned without one of these two.
